text:
<think>I should directly answer the question by identifying a developed country with much higher per capita seafood consumption than the US.</think>
Japan is a developed country that consumes significantly more seafood per person than the United States.
<think>I should provide a comparison to illustrate the difference in seafood consumption between Japan and the US.</think>
While the average American eats about 7 kilograms of seafood per year, the average Japanese person consumes more than 40 kilograms annually.
<think>I should mention that there are other developed countries with high seafood consumption, to show that Japan is not the only example.</think>
Other developed countries with high per capita seafood consumption include Norway, Spain, and Portugal.
<think>It would be helpful to briefly explain why seafood consumption is higher in these countries.</think>
Cultural preferences, geographic proximity to the ocean, and traditional diets all contribute to higher seafood consumption in these nations.
